prompt: I have two competing thoughts and I can't decide which one is wisdom vs. which one is hedging. Thought 1: 'I should keep my backup options open — Spotify, Pinterest, maybe even Google DeepMind. That way if frontier labs don't work out, I have a soft landing. It's prudent.' Thought 2: 'If I keep all these options alive, I'm diluting my focus and effort on Anthropic/OpenAI. I'll perform worse in interviews because my energy is split. I need to commit.' Which one is right? Or is this a false choice?

assertions.text: Distinguishes between hedging (fear) and real prudence (commitment + clear Plan B)
assertions.evidence: Coach clearly explains the difference and helps her see which she's actually doing

assertions.text: Maps to relevant principles (especially Brave, Work at the Frontier, Thoughtful)
assertions.evidence: Coach connects the decision to 3-4 core principles and shows where they point

assertions.text: Surfaces the real blocker underneath (identity threat, readiness anxiety, family risk)
assertions.evidence: Coach names the specific fears driving hedging and addresses each one

assertions.text: Pressure-tests her reasoning with specific clarifying questions
assertions.evidence: Coach asks questions that force her to think more deeply (would you actually take Spotify? What's the real cost of dilution?)

assertions.text: Lands on clear decision point: commit to frontier labs, stop maintaining warm connections
assertions.evidence: Action is specific and decision-based, not wishy-washy
